question: The free fall acceleration g increases as one proceeds at sea level, from the equator towards either pole. The reason is

en.content: The free fall acceleration g increases as one proceeds at sea level, from the equator towards either pole. The reason is

options.content: earth is a sphere with same density everywhere
earth is a sphere with different density at the polar regions than in the equatorial regions
earth is approximately an ellipsoid having its equatorial radius greater than its polar radius by 21 km
earth is approximately an ellipsoid having its equatorial radius smaller than its polar radius by 21 km.

en.explanation: Earth is not a sphere. Earth is approximately an ellipsoid, flattened at the pole and bulging at the equator. Its equatorial radius is greater than its polar radius by 21 km. Thus, a point at the pole is closer to the dense core of earth than a point on the equator. This is one reason, the free-fall acceleration g increases as one proceeds at sea level from the equator towards either the pole.

options: The balls will collide after 3s at a height of 30.2 m from the ground

question: Two balls, A and B are thrown simultaneously, A vertically upward with a speed of 20 m/s from the ground and B vertically downward from a height of 40 m with the same speed and along the same line of motion. At what points do the two balls collide by taking acceleration due to gravity as 9.8 m/s2 ?

en.content: Two balls, A and B are thrown simultaneously, A vertically upward with a speed of 20 m/s from the ground and B vertically downward from a height of 40 m with the same speed and along the same line of motion. At what points do the two balls collide by taking acceleration due to gravity as 9.8 m/s<sup>2</sup> ?

options.content: The balls will collide after 3s at a height of 30.2 m from the ground
The balls will collide after 2s at a height of 20.1 m from the ground
The balls will collide after 1s at a height of 15.1 m from the ground
The balls will collide after 5s at a height of 20 m from the ground

en.explanation:
Given, sped, u = 20 m/s, height, h = 40 m and acceleration due to gravity g = 9.8 m/s2

Then, from equation of motion,

$$s = ut + {1 \over 2}a{t^2}$$

Let, on point O after time t, O and C meet

Image

OC = 20t + $${1 \over 2}g{t^2}$$ ..... (i)

and OD = 20t $$-$$ $${1 \over 2}g{t^2}$$ ...... (ii)

OC + OD = CD = 40

Adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

40t = 40 $$\Rightarrow$$ t = 1s

$$\therefore$$ OD = 20 $$\times$$ 1 $$-$$ $${1 \over 2}$$ $$\times$$ 9.8 $$\times$$ 1 $$\times$$ 1 = 15.1 m

This shows that balls will collide after 1s at a height of 15.1 m from the ground.

en.title: NDA 2016 Paper 2
en.subTitle: Physics • General Science
en.excerpt: Two balls, A and B are thrown simultaneously, A vertically upward with a speed of 20 m/s from the ground and B vertically downward from a height of 40 m with the same speed and along the same line of motion. At what points do the two balls ...

solution:
Given, sped, u = 20 m/s, height, h = 40 m and acceleration due to gravity g = 9.8 m/s2

Then, from equation of motion,

$$s = ut + {1 \over 2}a{t^2}$$

Let, on point O after time t, O and C meet

Image

OC = 20t + $${1 \over 2}g{t^2}$$ ..... (i)

and OD = 20t $$-$$ $${1 \over 2}g{t^2}$$ ...... (ii)

OC + OD = CD = 40

Adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

40t = 40 $$\Rightarrow$$ t = 1s

$$\therefore$$ OD = 20 $$\times$$ 1 $$-$$ $${1 \over 2}$$ $$\times$$ 9.8 $$\times$$ 1 $$\times$$ 1 = 15.1 m

This shows that balls will collide after 1s at a height of 15.1 m from the ground.

question: Which of the following item is used in the household wirings to prevent accidental fire in case of short circuit?

en.content: Which of the following item is used in the household wirings to prevent accidental fire in case of short circuit?
en.explanation: An electrical fuse is a simple device used to interrupted an electrical circuit during over current condition due to short circuit and/or overload. An electrical fuse operates on the principle of heating effect of electrical current. When an over-current or short circuit condition occurs, which is the same as saying that the magnitude of current flowing in the circuit becomes higher than the rating of the fuse employed, its internal 'fuse wire' gets heated up to such an extent that it melts and breaks apart, which cuts off the supply of current to the circuit downstream. When this happens, the fuse is said to have 'blown'.

en.title: NDA 2016 Paper 2

When this happens, the fuse is said to have 'blown'.

A ray of light travelling from a rarer medium to a denser medium bends away from the normal.
question: Which one of the following statement is correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statement is correct?

options.content: The image formed by a concave mirror for an object lying at infinity is at the principal focus, highly diminished, real and inverted.
A ray of light parallel to the principal axis after reflection from a concave mirror appears to diverge from the principal focus of the mirror.
The focal length of a spherical mirror is double of its radius of curvature.
A ray of light travelling from a rarer medium to a denser medium bends away from the normal.

en.explanation: When an object is placed at infinity in front of a concave mirror, it will form a highly diminished, real and inverted image at focus of the mirror. Focal length of a spherical mirror is half of its radius of curvature. A ray of light coming parallel to the principal axis converges to focus of the spherical mirror after reflection from this mirror. During refraction, a ray of light travelling from a rarer medium to a denser medium bends towards the normal.

Lightning conductors are used to protect building from lightning strikes. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true about lightning conductors?

2. Lightning conductors reduce the effect of the strike by uniformly distributing the charge (current) over the surface of the building.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below

en.content:
Lightning conductors are used to protect building from lightning strikes. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true about lightning conductors?

1. Lightning conductors create an electric field at its top, so that lightning strikes it preferentially.

2. Lightning conductors reduce the effect of the strike by uniformly distributing the charge (current) over the surface of the building.

3. Lightning conductors take all charges (current) to deep down in the earth.

4. Lightning conductors must be installed at a place taller than the building.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below
en.explanation: Since, lightning conductors are at lower potential (as its wire is buried deep inside the earth), it attracts lightning (which is at the higher potential) and send them to deep down in the earth. Lightning conductors must be installed at a place taller than the building.

question:
Two forces, one of 3 N and another of 4 N are applied on a standard 1 kg body, placed on a horizontal and frictionless surface, simultaneously along the X-axis and the Y-axis, respectively, as shown below.

Image

en.content:
Two forces, one of 3 N and another of 4 N are applied on a standard 1 kg body, placed on a horizontal and frictionless surface, simultaneously along the X-axis and the Y-axis, respectively, as shown below.

Image

The magnitude of the resultant acceleration is
en.explanation:
As two forces are perpendicular to each other, so resultant force is given by

$${F_{net}} = \sqrt {F_1^2 + F_2^2} $$

$$ = \sqrt {{{(3)}^2} + {{(4)}^2}} $$

$$ = \sqrt {25} = 5$$ N

Now, from second law of Newton,

F = ma

Resultant acceleration, $${a_{net}} = {{{F_{net}}} \over m} = {5 \over 1} = 5$$ m/s2

question: The acceleration due to gravity 'g' for objects on or near the surface of earth is related to the universal gravitational constant 'G' as ('M' is the mass of the earth and 'R' is its radius)

en.content: The acceleration due to gravity 'g' for objects on or near the surface of earth is related to the universal gravitational constant 'G' as ('M' is the mass of the earth and 'R' is its radius)
en.explanation:
The acceleration due to gravity can be derived from law of gravitation.

Gravitational force between mass of earth (M) and a body of mass (m) is given by

$$F = {{GMm} \over {{R^2}}}$$ ..... (i)

where, R = radius of earth.

Again, we know earth attracts anybody with the force,

$$F = mg$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$${{GMm} \over {{R^2}}} = mg$$ or $$g = {{GM} \over {{R^2}}}$$

en.title: NDA 2015 Paper 2
en.subTitle: Physics • General Science
en.excerpt: The acceleration due to gravity 'g' for objects on or near the surface of earth is related to the universal gravitational constant 'G' as ('M' is the mass of the earth and 'R' is its radius)

solution:
The acceleration due to gravity can be derived from law of gravitation.

Gravitational force between mass of earth (M) and a body of mass (m) is given by

$$F = {{GMm} \over {{R^2}}}$$ ..... (i)

where, R = radius of earth.

Again, we know earth attracts anybody with the force,

$$F = mg$$ ..... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$${{GMm} \over {{R^2}}} = mg$$ or $$g = {{GM} \over {{R^2}}}$$

question: The loudness of sound is related to

en.content: The loudness of sound is related to
en.explanation: The amplitude of a sound wave determines its loudness. A larger amplitude means a louder sound and a smaller amplitude means a softer sound. Pitch and frequency are related with shrillness of sound.

question:
In case of a compound microscope, which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. The focal length of the eyepiece is larger than the focal length of the objective.

2. The focal length of the eyepiece is smaller than the focal length of the objective.

3. The image produced in a normal optical microscope is real.

4. The image produced in a normal optical microscope is virtual.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.

en.content:
In case of a compound microscope, which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. The focal length of the eyepiece is larger than the focal length of the objective.

2. The focal length of the eyepiece is smaller than the focal length of the objective.

3. The image produced in a normal optical microscope is real.

4. The image produced in a normal optical microscope is virtual.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
en.explanation: The focal length of the eyepiece is larger than the focal length of the objective and image produced in normal optical microscope is virtual. Focal length of objective is kept smaller to form a greatly enlarged image. This image is then viewed through eyepiece.

options: The radius of curvature of a concave mirror is twice its focal length.
Power of a convex lens is negative and that of a concave lens is positive.
The radius of curvature of a plane mirror is infinity.
When a ray of light passes from an optically denser medium to an optically rarer medium, an angle of refraction is greater than the corresponding angle of incidence.
question: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.content: The radius of curvature of a concave mirror is twice its focal length.
Power of a convex lens is negative and that of a concave lens is positive.
The radius of curvature of a plane mirror is infinity.
When a ray of light passes from an optically denser medium to an optically rarer medium, an angle of refraction is greater than the corresponding angle of incidence.

en.explanation:
Power of lens is given by $${1 \over {f(m)}}$$

For a concave lens 'f' is negative, so power will be negative and focal length of convex lens is positive, so its power will be positive.

options: If the velocity and acceleration have opposite sign, then the object is slowing down.
If the velocity is zero at an instant, then the acceleration should also be zero at that instant.
If the velocity is zero for a time interval, then the acceleration is zero at any instant within the time interval.
If the position and velocity have opposite sign, then the object is moving towards the origin.
question: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.content: If the velocity and acceleration have opposite sign, then the object is slowing down.
If the velocity is zero at an instant, then the acceleration should also be zero at that instant.
If the velocity is zero for a time interval, then the acceleration is zero at any instant within the time interval.
If the position and velocity have opposite sign, then the object is moving towards the origin.

en.explanation: During retarded motion, velocity and acceleration are in opposite directions. Therefore, we may have a situation, where velocity is zero but acceleration is not zero. e.g. in a spring block system velocity is zero at extreme position but acceleration is non-zero (maximum).

question: Three equal resistances when combined in series are equivalent to 90$$\Omega$$. Their equivalent resistance when combined in parallel will be

en.content: Three equal resistances when combined in series are equivalent to 90$$\Omega$$. Their equivalent resistance when combined in parallel will be
en.explanation:
Let each resistance be R.

According to the question,

R + R + R = 90$$\Omega$$

3R = 90$$\Omega$$ $$\Rightarrow$$ R = 30$$\Omega$$

where, these resistors are combined in parallel, then equivalent resistance is

$${R_{eq}} = {R \over 3} = {{30} \over 3} = 10\Omega $$

question:
The following figure represents the velocity-time graph of a moving car on a road.

Image

Which segment of the graph represents the retardation?

en.content:
The following figure represents the velocity-time graph of a moving car on a road.

Image

Which segment of the graph represents the retardation?
en.explanation:
Rate of decrease of velocity with time is called retardation. Therefore, during retardation velocity of the car should decreases with time. Pitch and frequency are related with shrillness of sound.

Thus, segment BC represents retardation as velocity of the car decreases with time.

options: The Kelvin scale of temperature is called the absolute scale.
Visible light radiation has wavelength range of 400-700 nm.
The capacity to do work is called power.
The wavelength of $$\gamma$$-rays is less than the wavelength of X-rays.
question: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.content: The Kelvin scale of temperature is called the absolute scale.
Visible light radiation has wavelength range of 400-700 nm.
The capacity to do work is called power.
The wavelength of $$\gamma$$-rays is less than the wavelength of X-rays.

en.explanation:
Capacity of doing work is called energy and the rate of doing work is called power.

Wavelength of X-rays is in the order of 10$$-$$10 m or 1 $$\mathop A\limits^o $$. Wavelength of $$\gamma$$-rays is in the order of 10$$-$$12 m. Therefore, the wavelength of $$\gamma$$-rays is less than the wavelength of X-rays.

question: A man is sitting in a train which is moving with a velocity of 60 km/h. His speed with respect to the train is

en.content: A man is sitting in a train which is moving with a velocity of 60 km/h. His speed with respect to the train is
en.explanation:
According to the concept of relative velocity, both the train and man moves with the velocity of 60 km/h.

So, velocity of man w.r.t. train will be velocity of man $$-$$ velocity of train, i.e. 60 $$-$$ 60 = 0 (zero)

question: Which one of the following is the SI unit of the thermal conductivity of a material?

en.content: Which one of the following is the SI unit of the thermal conductivity of a material?
en.explanation:
Thermal conductivity of a material is the amount of heat required per second to increase the temperature of unit length of the material having unit cross-section through 1$$^\circ$$C or 1 K.

Therefore, unit of thermal conductivity is Wm$$-$$1K$$-$$1.

question: The resistance of a wire of length l and area of cross-section a is x $$\Omega$$. If the wire is stretched to double its length, then its resistance would become

en.content: The resistance of a wire of length l and area of cross-section a is x $$\Omega$$. If the wire is stretched to double its length, then its resistance would become
en.explanation:
According to the question, resistance of wire is given by

$$x = {{\rho l} \over a}$$

(where, $$\rho$$ = resistivity, l = length and a = area of cross-section)

If length is doubled, its area of cross-section decreases but volume remains constant.

i.e. l $$\times$$ a = 2l $$\times$$ A' (here, A' = new area)

$$ \Rightarrow A' = {a \over 2}$$

Now, new resistance,

$$R = {{\rho 2l} \over {a/2}} = {{4\rho l} \over a}$$

As $${{\rho l} \over a} = x\,\Omega $$

So, R = 4x $$\Omega$$
